generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor receiving input current (I_M) and producing motor torque (τ_M) as part of the MABI MAX 100 first axis drivetrain."
]

instance GearTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gear transmission receiving motor torque (τ_M) and transmitting gearwheel torque (τ_G) to the cycloidal gearbox."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox receiving gearwheel torque (τ_G) and producing system output torque (τ_out) with integrated friction dynamics."
]

instance SINUMERIK840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller generating motor current (I_M) based on position/speed/acceleration control inputs for the first axis."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor measuring angular position and velocity (ω_M) for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder mounted behind the gearbox measuring output angular position and velocity (ω_out) to enable backlash and elasticity compensation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring motor input current (I_M) which is converted to motor torque (τ_M) using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentSensorDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series data of motor angular position and velocity (ω_M) streamed via MQTT protocol for real-time friction analysis."
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
    base:desc "Time-series data of output angular position and velocity (ω_out) streamed via MQTT protocol to detect backlash and elasticity effects."
]

instance CurrentSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
    base:desc "Time-series data of motor input current (I_M) streamed via MQTT protocol for torque derivation and friction modeling."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLMotorStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (bdd) defining the structural components and flow ports (rotational, current) of the permanent magnet synchronous motor."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance SysMLGearboxStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (bdd) defining the structural components and flow ports of the gear transmission and cycloidal gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance SysMLFirstAxisComposition : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (ibd) composing the first axis as a system (Motor → Gearwheel → Cycloidal Gearbox) with defined interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool
]

instance MotorTorqueVelocityModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data calculation model deriving motor torque (τ_M) and velocity (ω_M) from input current (I_M) using manufacturer-provided motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingFramework
]

instance GearboxDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamics model simulating gearwheel output torque (τ_G) and velocity (ω_G) based on motor input and gearbox parameters."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingFramework
]

instance SystemOutputModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamics model simulating system output torque (τ_out) and velocity (ω_out) derived from gearbox output and friction effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingFramework
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics with configurable parameters for static and kinetic friction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingFramework
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink friction model implementing Stribeck curve with configurable parameters for boundary, mixed, and fluid lubrication regimes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingFramework
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink friction model implementing Dahl's friction model for pre-sliding displacement and friction lag effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingFramework
]

instance LuGreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink friction model implementing LuGre model for high-accuracy friction representation including bristle deformation and wear effects."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingFramework
]

instance FrictionlessBaselineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink baseline model representing ideal gearbox behavior without friction for comparative analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkProcessingFramework
]

instance MeasuredMotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of measured motor input current (I_M) with metadata including time stamps and system properties."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentSensorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorTorqueVelocityModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel
]

instance DerivedMotorTorqueVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Calculated motor torque (τ_M) and velocity (ω_M) derived from I_M using motor constants with metadata linking to source current data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredMotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GearboxDynamicsModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel
]

instance MeasuredMotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of motor angular position and velocity (ω_M) from the motor encoder with metadata including time stamps."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GearboxDynamicsModel, FrictionlessBaselineModel
]

instance MeasuredOutputEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data of output angular position and velocity (ω_out) from the secondary encoder with metadata including time stamps."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SystemOutputModel, FrictionlessBaselineModel
]

instance SimulatedGearwheelTorqueVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated gearwheel torque (τ_G) and velocity (ω_G) derived from motor input and gearbox dynamics models with metadata."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DerivedMotorTorqueVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SystemOutputModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LuGreFrictionModel
]

instance SimulatedSystemOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated system output torque (τ_out) and velocity (ω_out) incorporating friction effects with metadata for comparison."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulatedGearwheelTorqueVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionAnalysisService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML toolset for creating structural and behavioral models (Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams) of the robot's first axis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelCreationService, SystemCompositionService
]

instance SimulinkProcessingFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink framework for executing friction and dynamics simulations with configurable model parameters."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionSimulationService, MultiPhysicsSimulationService, ModelAdaptationService
]

instance MQTTDataStreaming : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time streaming of sensor data (I_M, encoder angles) from the robot to the digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataAcquisitionService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) for semantic linking of data traces to their sources, models, and processing steps with metadata."
]

instance ProcessingComponentSimulationM : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component consuming motor current (I_M) and encoder data to produce motor torque/velocity derivatives via Simulink models."
]

instance ProcessingComponentSimulationGW : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component simulating gearwheel torque/velocity (τ_G, ω_G) from motor input and gearbox dynamics."
]

instance ProcessingComponentSimulationGB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component simulating system output torque/velocity (τ_out, ω_out) incorporating friction models."
]

instance RealTimeDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard for real-time visualization of simulated vs. measured data deviations, friction model outputs, and system properties."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionVisualizationService, DeviationAnalysisService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionModelCreationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service creating white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models in Simulink with configurable parameters."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionModelInsight
]

instance SystemCompositionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service composing the first axis as a system using SysML Internal Block Diagrams with defined interfaces between components."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemCompositionInsight
]

instance FrictionSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) in Simulink to simulate friction-inclusive behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionSimulationInsight
]

instance MultiPhysicsSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service simulating frictionless baseline and friction-inclusive behavior for comparative analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides MultiPhysicsSimulationInsight
]

instance RealTimeDataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service acquiring real-time sensor data (I_M, encoder angles) via MQTT streaming for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDataInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service combining simulated and measured data to identify friction losses (static, kinetic, viscous) via deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossInsight, ControlAccuracyImprovementAction
]

instance DataTraceabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service linking data traces to their sources, processing models, and time intervals via Asset Administration Shell."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

instance ModelAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously updating friction models using live data to adapt to wear, temperature, or load changes."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAdaptationInsight
]

instance FrictionVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service displaying deviations between simulated and measured data in real-time dashboards."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionVisualizationInsight
]

instance DeviationAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing deviations between simulated and measured data to quantify friction-induced errors."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviationAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the structural and behavioral definition of friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre) for the gearbox."
]

instance SystemCompositionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the composed system structure of the first axis (Motor → Gearwheel → Cycloidal Gearbox) with defined interfaces."
]

instance FrictionSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the simulated friction behavior using configurable parameters for different friction models."
]

instance MultiPhysicsSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the comparison between frictionless baseline and friction-inclusive behavior for error quantification."
]

instance RealTimeDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the real-time acquisition of sensor data (I_M, encoder angles) for immediate friction analysis."
]

instance FrictionLossInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into detected friction losses (static, kinetic, viscous) via deviation analysis between simulated and measured data."
]

instance ControlAccuracyImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to refine friction models for real-time control adjustments to improve motion accuracy by up to 30%."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the semantic linking of data traces to their sources, models, and processing steps with metadata."
]

instance ModelAdaptationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the continuous adaptation of friction models to account for wear, temperature, or load changes using live data."
]

instance FrictionVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the real-time visualization of simulated vs. measured data deviations and friction model outputs."
]

instance DeviationAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into the quantified friction-induced errors derived from deviation analysis between simulated and measured data."
]

// C1: Environment (System Under Study)
instance MABIMAXFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Environment representing the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot's first vertical axis with working range [-90°, 90°] and max speed 75°/s."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearTransmission, CycloidalGearbox, SINUMERIK840DslController,
                 MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, CurrentSensor
]

// C5: Components (Physical Acting/Sensing)
instance FirstAxisDrivetrainComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical component representing the first axis drivetrain of the MABI MAX 100 robot including motor, gear transmission, and cycloidal gearbox."
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAXFirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance CNCControlComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical component representing the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller generating actuation signals for the first axis."
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAXFirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance SensorArrayComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical component representing the sensing infrastructure for the first axis including encoders and current sensor."
    base:isContainedIn MABIMAXFirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** is **one-way passive** (as per the Digital Shadow definition), meaning the DS does **not** directly actuate the physical system. However, the DS enables:
  1. **Indirect influence** via **insights and recommendations** for **control parameter updates** (e.g., friction compensation in the CNC controller).
  2. **Visualization of deviations** between simulated (friction-inclusive) and measured data in a **dashboard**, aiding human operators in **adjusting control strategies**.
  3. **Traceable data provenance**: The DS provides **metadata** (e.g., model parameters, time stamps) to support **root-cause analysis** of friction-related inaccuracies, which can inform **offline control tuning** or **model updates** for future operations."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital shadow operates in **real-time** with **live data** during robot operation. Data traces (e.g., current, torque, angular velocity) are continuously streamed via MQTT and processed to update friction models dynamically. The system is designed for **operational synchronization**, ensuring that the digital shadow reflects the current state of the physical system for immediate friction analysis."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **DT constellation** is a **purpose-specific Digital Shadow** with the following orchestration:
  1. **Centralized Architecture**:
     - **Single DS instance** per first axis, with **modular components** (models, data traces, processing units).
  2. **Data Flow**:
     - **Physical → Virtual**: MQTT-streamed sensor data → Processing components → Simulink simulations → Dashboard.
     - **Virtual → Virtual**: Data traces are **linked transitively** via **originates-from associations** (e.g., simulated *τ_G* depends on measured *I_M* and friction model).
  3. **Service Orchestration**:
     - **Friction Derivation Service** (core purpose) orchestrates:
       - **Data acquisition** (MQTT).
       - **Model execution** (Simulink).
       - **Visualization** (dashboard).
       - **Traceability** (AAS metadata).
  4. **Extensibility**:
     - Designed to integrate with **higher-level Digital Twin systems** (e.g., for full robot or manufacturing cell control) via **standardized interfaces** (e.g., AAS, OPC UA)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The **life-cycle stages** covered by the DS include:
  1. **As-operated phase**: The DS focuses on **real-time monitoring and analysis** of the **first axis during operation**, capturing **dynamic friction behavior** under varying loads, temperatures, and wear.
  2. **Representation fidelity**: The DS provides a **behavioral twin** (not a **geometric twin**) with **multi-fidelity models**:
     - **High-fidelity**: Simulink simulations of friction dynamics.
     - **Low-fidelity**: Structural SysML models for component interconnections.
  3. **Adaptive evolution**: The DS supports **continuous model updates** to account for **wear, tool changes, or environmental shifts** (e.g., temperature), ensuring **long-term validity** of friction estimates."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity is ensured through:
    - **Structural models** (SysML) describing the physical components and their interconnections.
    - **Computable models** (Simulink) simulating friction and system dynamics with high precision.
    Validity is validated by comparing **simulated data traces** (ˆ*r_M*, ˆ*r_G*, ˆ*r_out*) against **measured data traces** (*r_M*, *r_G*, *r_out*) to detect deviations caused by friction. The system does not explicitly mention metrics like RMSE but relies on **traceability** to ensure data integrity and model accuracy."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital twin connects to the physical system via **MQTT** for real-time sensor data streaming. The robot's **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl** controller provides access to sensor data (current, position), which is processed in the digital shadow. No specific network protocols (e.g., OPC UA) or architectures (e.g., edge computing) are detailed beyond MQTT and the controller interface."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is deployed as an **executable system** integrating Simulink models and a dashboard for visualization. Hosting details are not specified beyond the use of **Simulink for simulations** and a **dashboard for data presentation**. No mention of specific technologies (e.g., Docker, InfluxDB) or hardware (e.g., x86 GPU) is provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The digital shadow integrates with:
    - The **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl** controller for sensor data access.
    - **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)** for semantic data linking.
    No details are provided on external systems (e.g., OPC UA, cloud platforms) or API-based exchanges beyond these integrations."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital shadow adheres to:
    - **SysML** for systems modeling.
    - **MQTT** for real-time data streaming.
    - **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** for conceptual framework.
    - **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies.
    No specific standard numbers (e.g., IEC 62541) or organizations (e.g., OMG) are mentioned."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital shadow is a **passive entity** with no autonomous operations or direct physical interactions, thus minimizing safety risks. It does not execute remote operations on the physical system. No cybersecurity measures (e.g., TLS, authentication) or operational safety protocols are detailed in the documents."
]
